articles.title: US-Iran indirect talks in Doha make 'positive progress'; next round set for July 18 after Khamenei funeral
articles.summary: Qatar-mediated indirect talks between US and Iranian negotiators wrapped up in Doha with 'positive progress' on issues tied to the Islamabad Memorandum of Understanding, per Qatar's foreign ministry. Al Arabiya sources say an initial agreement was reached to release $3 billion to Iran, and the next round is expected July 18, after the late supreme leader's funeral.

articles.title: Iran prepares massive state funeral for slain Supreme Leader Khamenei, July 4-9
articles.summary: Iran is preparing multi-day funeral ceremonies for Ali Khamenei, killed in an airstrike on the war's first day (Feb 28), running July 4-9 across Tehran, Qom and Mashhad in Iran and Najaf and Karbala in Iraq. Officials expect 15-20 million mourners; parliament speaker Ghalibaf called for a massive turnout to 'avenge' his death. Whether new supreme leader Mojtaba Khamenei will lead prayers is unclear. Tehran warned the US and Israel against attacks during the ceremonies.

articles.title: Gaza war's 1,000 days: 90% of strip 'destroyed', 80% 'seized' by Israel
articles.summary: As the war reaches 1,000 days, Gaza authorities say more than 90 percent of the strip is destroyed and Israeli forces control 80 percent. Gaza's Government Media Office puts deaths at 73,066, including over 21,500 children; 9,500 are missing. The US-created Board of Peace, set up in January to oversee the ceasefire and reconstruction, has failed to secure Israeli compliance, with only a third of committed aid trucks entering daily.

articles.title: Israel marks 1,000 days since October 7; thousands at Hostages Square demand commission of inquiry
articles.summary: Israel marked 1,000 days since the October 7, 2023 attack with memorial ceremonies and protests. Thousands gathered at Hostages Square in Tel Aviv demanding a state commission of inquiry; protesters also demonstrated outside the Knesset and IDF headquarters. President Herzog marked the milestone with Kerem Shalom survivors.

articles.title: Bomb blast at Damascus cafe kills five, injures 16
articles.summary: A bomb planted at a crowded cafe near the Palace of Justice in central Damascus killed at least five people and wounded 16, Syrian state TV reported. Authorities are investigating; no group immediately claimed responsibility.

articles.title: Syria and Lebanon form joint ministerial committee; Damascus 'open to meeting Hezbollah'
articles.summary: Syrian FM Asaad Al-Shaibani and Lebanese PM Nawaf Salam signed an agreement in Beirut establishing a joint ministerial committee on security coordination and economic cooperation. During the visit, Al-Shaibani said Damascus is open to meeting Hezbollah 'if interests require it', after Trump suggested Syria should fight the group. He also met President Aoun and speaker Berri.

articles.title: US tells Tehran it rejects any changes to Strait of Hormuz status quo
articles.summary: After the Doha talks concluded, Washington sent Tehran clear messages rejecting any change to the current status quo in the Strait of Hormuz, saying any change would be considered a violation of the agreement, per Al Arabiya sources. Iran in turn warned the US against interference in the strait. The MoU obliges Iran to ensure toll-free passage for 60 days pending a final deal.

articles.title: Strait of Hormuz to remain 'Warlike Operations Area' until at least July 9; unions warn of seafarer risk
articles.summary: The International Transport Workers' Federation and the Joint Negotiating Group agreed to keep the Strait of Hormuz classified as a Warlike Operations Area until July 9, citing significant risk to seafarers. The IMO has logged 49 confirmed incidents in the strait and wider region; 34 vessel crossings were verified on June 30 as traffic partially recovers.

articles.title: Hamas calls for emergency Arab summit over alleged Gaza displacement plans
articles.summary: Hamas called on the Arab League to convene an emergency summit over what it described as Israeli plans to displace Palestinians from Gaza, urging Arab states to adopt a unified political response. The call follows Netanyahu's comments that 'voluntary migration' remains an option.

articles.title: Trump's Board of Peace says UNRWA has 'no place' in new Gaza
articles.summary: The US-created Board of Peace overseeing Gaza's ceasefire framework said UNRWA 'has no place in new Gaza', deepening questions about aid delivery as the agency remains the largest humanitarian provider for Palestinian refugees.

articles.title: IDF says strike killed Hezbollah operative who emerged from tunnel in south Lebanon
articles.summary: The Israeli military said an airstrike killed a Hezbollah operative who emerged from an underground facility at the Ali Taher ridge in southern Lebanon, days after Israel and Lebanon signed a framework agreement that Hezbollah has condemned.

articles.title: B'Tselem: 54 Palestinian children killed in West Bank in 2025 'not isolated incidents'
articles.summary: Israeli human rights group B'Tselem reported that Israeli forces killed 54 Palestinian children in the West Bank in 2025, saying the killings were not isolated incidents but part of a broader pattern.

articles.title: Israel and US launch talks on new defense framework to replace 10-year security MoU
articles.summary: Israel and the United States held a kickoff meeting to launch formal talks on a new bilateral defense framework to replace the current 10-year security assistance Memorandum of Understanding, which expires in 2028.

articles.title: US indirectly warned Iran that Israel might try to kill its top negotiators during war-ending talks, NYT reports
articles.summary: Citing a New York Times report, US officials indirectly warned Iranian FM Abbas Araghchi and parliament speaker Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf during the war-ending negotiations that Israel might attempt to assassinate them.
